      • writeDisposition(buf: ptr UncheckedArray[byte]; name: string; p: var int)

A non-blocking HTTP/1.1 server built on top of powpow's TCP primitives.

Combines the TCP transport layer with the incremental HTTP parser to provide a high-level routing server with minimal overhead.

Usage: let server = newHttpServer(loop) server.get("/") do (req: HttpRequest, res: Response): res.status(Http200).send("Hello!") server.listen("0.0.0.0", 8080) loop.run()

Imports

../net/tcp, ../net/common, ../loop, ../types, http

Types

BodyHandler = proc (data: openArray[byte]; done: bool) {.closure.}

Streaming body handler. Receives body data as it arrives from the network. done is true when the body is complete.

Use with streaming routes to process large uploads without buffering: server.post("/upload", bodyHandler = cb) do (req, res): ...

Handler = proc (req: HttpRequest; res: Response) {.closure.}
Route handler. Call res.status(...).send(...) to respond. 
HttpServer = ref object
A non-blocking HTTP/1.1 server. 
Response = ref object
Build and send an HTTP response.

Consts

DefaultChunkSize = 1048576
DefaultKeepAliveMs = 5000

Procs

proc addConnection(server: HttpServer; fd: SocketHandle) {.
    ...raises: [Exception, OSError], tags: [RootEffect], forbids: [].}
Inject a pre-accepted client fd into this HTTP server's event loop. Used by multi-threaded acceptors that distribute connections to workers. 
proc close(res: Response): Response {.discardable, ...raises: [], tags: [],
                                      forbids: [].}
Mark this response to send "Connection: close" and shut down the TCP connection after the response is sent. 
proc close(server: HttpServer) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Shut down the server. 
proc delete(server: HttpServer; path: string; bodyHandler: BodyHandler;
            handler: Handler) {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a DELETE route handler with a streaming body handler. 
proc delete(server: HttpServer; path: string; handler: Handler) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a DELETE route handler. 
proc delete(server: HttpServer; path: string; handler: Handler;
            streamToFile: bool) {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a DELETE route that streams the raw body to a temp file. 
proc ensureTcpServer(server: HttpServer) {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Lazily create the underlying TcpServer (for multi-thread use where listen() is called before routes are registered). 
proc get(server: HttpServer; path: string; handler: Handler) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a GET route handler. 
proc getConn(res: Response): Connection {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Get the underlying TCP connection. Used by protocol upgrade handlers (e.g. WebSocket) that need direct access to the socket. 
proc getFileExt(path: string): string {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Extract the lowercase file extension from a path. 
proc getLoop(server: HttpServer): Loop {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Get the event loop associated with this server. 
proc head(server: HttpServer; path: string; handler: Handler) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a HEAD route handler. 
proc header(res: Response; key, value: string): Response {.discardable,
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Add a response header. 
proc listen(server: HttpServer; address: string; port: int) {.
    ...raises: [NetError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Bind and start accepting HTTP connections on a TCP port. 
proc listenUnix(server: HttpServer; path: string; mode: int = 0o000000000660) {.
    ...raises: [NetError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Bind and start accepting HTTP connections on a Unix domain socket. 
proc markSent(res: Response) {.inline, ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Mark this response as sent without writing any bytes. Used by upgrade handlers that send the response manually. 
proc newHttpServer(loop: Loop): HttpServer {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Create a new HTTP server. 
proc notFound(server: HttpServer; handler: Handler) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Register a catch-all handler for unmatched routes. 
proc options(server: HttpServer; path: string; handler: Handler) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register an OPTIONS route handler. 
proc parseRange(rangeHeader: string; fileSize: int64): tuple[ok: bool,
    start, length: int64] {....raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Parse an HTTP Range header (single range, bytes=start-end). Supports explicit ranges (0-1023), open-ended ranges (1024-), and suffix ranges (-500). Clamps rangeEnd to fileSize - 1. Returns (true, startByte, length) on success, (false, 0, 0) on failure. 
proc patch(server: HttpServer; path: string; bodyHandler: BodyHandler;
           handler: Handler) {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a PATCH route handler with a streaming body handler. 
proc patch(server: HttpServer; path: string; handler: Handler) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a PATCH route handler. 
proc patch(server: HttpServer; path: string; handler: Handler;
           streamToFile: bool) {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a PATCH route that streams the raw body to a temp file. 
proc post(server: HttpServer; path: string; bodyHandler: BodyHandler;
          handler: Handler) {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a POST route handler with a streaming body handler. 
proc post(server: HttpServer; path: string; handler: Handler) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a POST route handler. 
proc post(server: HttpServer; path: string; streamToFile: bool; handler: Handler) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a POST route that streams the raw body to a temp file. Memory: ~68KB regardless of body size. 
proc put(server: HttpServer; path: string; bodyHandler: BodyHandler;
         handler: Handler) {....raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a PUT route handler with a streaming body handler. 
proc put(server: HttpServer; path: string; handler: Handler) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a PUT route handler. 
proc put(server: HttpServer; path: string; streamToFile: bool; handler: Handler) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a PUT route that streams the raw body to a temp file. 
proc removeSession(server: HttpServer; fd: int) {....raises: [KeyError, OSError],
    tags: [WriteDirEffect], forbids: [].}
Clean up a connection's session. Public so protocol upgrade handlers (e.g. WebSocket) can take over a connection. 
proc route(server: HttpServer; meth: HttpMethod; path: string;
           bodyHandler: BodyHandler; handler: Handler) {....raises: [KeyError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a route handler with a streaming body handler. The body handler receives body data as it arrives from the network. When the body is complete, done is set to true. 
proc route(server: HttpServer; meth: HttpMethod; path: string; handler: Handler) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Register a route handler. 
proc send(res: Response; body: seq[byte]) {....raises: [KeyError, OSError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Send the response with a raw byte body. Uses scatter-write (writev) to send headers + body with zero intermediate string allocations. 
proc send(res: Response; body: string = "") {....raises: [KeyError, OSError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Send the response with a string body. Uses scatter-write (writev) to send headers + body with zero intermediate string allocations. 
proc sendError(res: Response; code: HttpCode; msg: string = "") {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Send an error response and close the connection. 
proc sendFile(res: Response; path: string;
              req: HttpRequest = default(HttpRequest); closeConn = true) {.
    ...raises: [KeyError, OSError], tags: [], forbids: [].}
Send a file for download using zero-copy when possible. Adds Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="...". Supports HTTP Range requests when req is provided. closeConn controls connection lifetime: true (default) closes after the transfer; false keeps the connection alive. 
proc setKeepAliveTimeout(server: HttpServer; ms: int) {....raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
Set the keep-alive idle timeout in milliseconds. 0 disables it. 
proc status(res: Response; code: HttpCode): Response {.discardable, ...raises: [],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}
Set the HTTP status code. 
proc streamFile(res: Response; path: string; req: HttpRequest;
                chunkSize = DefaultChunkSize) {....raises: [KeyError, OSError],
    tags: [], forbids: [].}

Stream a file for media playback with per-response byte limiting. Always process Range requests. Caps each response body to chunkSize bytes (default 1 MB) so a seek only transfers one chunk, not the entire remaining file. Always uses keep-alive.

No initial (no-Range) request sends 206 with Content-Range: bytes 0-(chunkSize-1)/fileSize — the browser learns the total file size from the suffix but only receives one chunk.

proc writeDisposition(buf: ptr UncheckedArray[byte]; name: string; p: var int) {.
    ...raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
